chapter_title.en: Chapter 100 – The sheep given out (for pasture) with the tribute payment

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/260)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/260)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ الْغَنَمُ يُعْطِيهَا بِضَرِيبَةٍ سَمْناً شَيْئاً مَعْلُوماً أَوْ دَرَاهِمَ مَعْلُومَةً مِنْ كُلِّ شَاةٍ كَذَا وَ كَذَا قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِالدَّرَاهِمِ وَ لَسْتُ أُحِبُّ أَنْ يَكُونَ بِالسَّمْنِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who happens to have the sheep for him, he gives it out (for pasture) with a tribute of butter, something known, or a known amount of Dirhams, from every sheep such and such. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is not problem with the Dirhams, and I<sup>asws</sup> do not like it if it happens to be with the butter’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “About the case of a man who owns sheep and gives them to be maintained and paid for their maintenance from their ghee a certain amount or a known amount of dirham for every sheep, abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Dirham is not unlawful but I do not like giving ghee.’”
